person.name: Geoffrey de Saye
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Geoffrey de Saye, son and heir of William de Saye, Knt., and Elizabeth ______, was born about 1279-1281 (aged 14 or 16 in 1295, proved age on or soon before 15 February 1302/3).

Geoffrey married after 28 December 1295 (date of his marriage grant) and before 1305 to Idoine de Leybourne, daughter of William de Leybourne, Knt., Lord Leybourne and Juliane, daughter and heiress of Sir Henry de Sandwich. 

«b»They had three sons and three daughters:«/b»
1.) Geoffrey, Knt., 2nd Lord Say born 30 April 1305, died 26 June 1359, married Maud de Beauchamo

2.) Roger, probably the Roger de Say who was deputy 1353-1360 for Geoffrey de Say, Constable of Rochester Castle

3.) Ralph, living 31 March 1321

4.) Katherine, who married Sir John de Saint John, 3rd Lord St. John of Lageham

5.) Juliane, married Sir Roger de Northwode, 2nd Lord Northwode

6.) Isabel (or Elizabeth), married John de Chaumpaigne

Geoffrey was continually employed in the wars in Scotland. In 1308 he attended the Coronation in the train of the King and Queen. He was summoned to Parliament from 26 July 1313 to 14 May 1321 by writs directed Galfrido de Say, whereby he is held to have become Lord Say.

Geoffrey died before 3 March 1321/2. Before his death, he had settled his manor of West Greenwich on himself and Idoine, "with successive remainders to their sons Geoffrey, Roger and Ralph and the heirs of their bodies". Idoine was last recorded living in 14 November 1337.

«b»Research Notes
Manor of Saye's Court«/b»
"William his son succeeded him, and died anno 56 Henry III. holding this manor in capite by barony, and the repair of a house in Dover-castle, called, from its possessors, Saye's-tower. His son of the same name, accounted at the Exchequer for twenty-seven fees of the honour of Magminot; that is, twenty-six of the old feoffment and one of the new, which shews the large extent of his possessions. He died anno 23 king Edward I. possessed of this manor, leaving Geoffrey his son and heir,  who married Idonea, daughter of William de Leyborne (who survived him) and died possessed of it, in the 15th of king Edward II. leaving Geoffrey de Saye, his son, who, in the 8th of king Edward III. obtained the king's charter for free warren for all his demean lands in his lordships of Greenwich, Deptford, &c. with the view of frankpledge and other privileges, and died in the 33d year of it, leaving William his son and heir, and Maud his wife, surviving, (daughter of Guy de Beauchamp, earl of Warwick) and three daughters, who afterwards became their brother's heirs, as will be further mentioned."

«b»Sawbridgeworth Manor«/b»
"Geoffrey de Saye, his son, was summoned to Parliament as Lord Say from 1313. In 1306 he obtained a renewal of the Friday market granted in 1223 and a grant of a yearly fair on the vigil and feast of the Nativity of the Virgin Mary (8 September).  He died in 1322; his wife Idonia survived him and received a grant of the manor for life from her son Geoffrey.  Geoffrey the younger died in 1359 and was succeeded by his son William de Say. The extent of the manor taken at his death in 1375 gives a messuage with garden, 500 acres of arable land, 15 acres of meadow, 20 acres of pasture and 100 acres of wood. The rents from customary tenants included 1 lb. of wax and 3 gross of arrows. John son and heir of William died a minor in 1382. The manor passed to his sister Elizabeth, who made a settlement on herself and her first husband, Sir John de Falwesle, in 1388, and on herself and second husband, Sir William Heron, in 1396."

«b»Birling Manor«/b»
"His son, Geoffry de Say, was summoned to parliament, among the barons of this realm in the 7th year of king Edward II. as were his several descendants afterwards. He died in the 15th year of that reign, being then possessed of this manor, which he held of the king in capite, as of the barony of Maminot. At length John de Say dying in his minority, and in ward to the king in the 6th year of king Richard II Elizabeth his sister, became his heir, and possessed of this manor. She married first Sir John de Fallesley, by whom she had no issue, and afterwards Sir William Heron, who possessed this manor and bore the title of lord Say in her right."

zh.Rocester, Kent, England: 英國英格蘭斯塔福爾洛斯特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Geoffrey de Saye於1281年4月1日出生在英國艾可艾爾納姆時，他的父親William de Saye IV27歲，母親Elizabeth24歲。他於1295年12月28日與Idoine Leybourne在英國艾可結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1322年3月3日在他的家鄉享年40歲。

zh-hans.Rocester, Kent, England: 英国英格兰斯塔福爾洛斯特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Geoffrey de Saye于1281年4月1日出生在英国艾可艾爾納姆时，他的父亲William de Saye IV27岁，母亲Elizabeth24岁。他于1295年12月28日与Idoine Leybourne在英国艾可结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1322年3月3日在他的家乡享年40岁。

nameMeanings.name: Saye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Slovenian Saje: nickname for someone with very dark, sooty hair, derived from the root of sajast ‘sooty’, or directly from saja ‘soot’.
West African (Liberia): from the personal name Saye, found among the Mano (Mah) people, denoting a first-born boy.

variants.name: Say
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Sai in Orne or Say in Indre, France, perhaps so called from a Gaulish personal name Saius + the Latin locative suffix -acum (see also French name 3 below).

English: nickname, perhaps for someone who made or sold say, a type of woollen cloth, or for an habitual wearer of clothes made of this material, from Middle English sai(e) ‘say’ (Old French saie, from Latin saga, plural of sagum ‘military cloak’). See also Sayer .

French (southern): topographic name from saix (from Latin saxum) ‘rock’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example, Say in Loire, Saix in Tarn and Vienne, Le Saix in Hautes-Alpes, or Les Saix in Isère.

nameMeanings.name: Geoffrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic (Frankish and Lombard) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It was in regular use among the counts of Anjou, ancestors of the English royal house of Plantagenet, who were descended from Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou ( 1113–51 ). It was a particularly popular name in England and France in the later Middle Ages; notable bearers in England include the poet Geoffrey Chaucer ( c.1340–1400 ) and in Wales the chronicler Geoffrey of Monmouth ( d. 1155 ). The original form and meaning of the name is disputed. According to one theory, it is merely a variant of Godfrey ; others derive the first part from the Germanic word gawia ‘territory’, walah ‘stranger’, or gisil ‘pledge’. Medieval forms can be found to support all these theories, and it is possible that several names have fallen together, or that the name was subjected to reanalysis by folk etymology at an early date.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Geoffrey de Saye was born on 1 April 1281, in Elsenham, Essex, England, his father, William de Saye IV, was 27 and his mother, Elizabeth, was 24. He married Idoine Leybourne on 28 December 1295, in Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 3 March 1322, in his hometown, at the age of 40.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Geoffrey de Saye est né le 1 avril 1281, à Elsenham, Essex, Angleterre, son père, William de Saye IV, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Idoine Leybourne le 28 décembre 1295, à Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 3 mars 1322, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 40 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Geoffrey de Saye am 1. April 1281 in Elsenham, Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Saye IV 27 und seine Mutter Elizabeth 24. Er hat am 28. Dezember 1295 in Essex, England Idoine Leybourne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 3. März 1322 im Alter von 40 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Geoffrey de Saye è nato il il 1 aprile 1281 nel Elsenham, Essex, England, il suo padre, William de Saye IV, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Idoine Leybourne il 28 dicembre 1295, nel Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 3 marzo 1322, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 40 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Sayeが1281年4月1日にイギリスエセックスエルセンハムで生まれた時，父William de Saye IVは27歳，母Elizabethは24歳であった。1295年12月28日にイギリスエセックスでIdoine Leybourneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1322年3月3日に故郷で40歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Saye(이)가 1281년 4월 1일 영국 에세스 엘센함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Saye IV(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1295년 12월 28일 영국 에세스에서 Idoine Leybourne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1322년 3월 3일 그의 고향에서 40세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.1 April 1281–3 March 1322: 1 de abril de 1281–3 de março de 1322
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Geoffrey de Saye nasceu em 1 de abril de 1281, em Elsenham, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, William de Saye IV, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Idoine Leybourne em 28 de dezembro de 1295, em Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 3 de março de 1322, em sua cidade natal, com 40 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Geoffrey de Saye родился 1 апреля 1281 г. в местности элсенам, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени William de Saye IV было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Idoine Leybourne 28 декабря 1295 г. в местности эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Он умер 3 марта 1322 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 40 лет.

es.1 April 1281–3 March 1322: 1 de abril de 1281–3 de marzo de 1322
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Geoffrey de Saye nació el 1 de abril de 1281, en Elsenham, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, William de Saye IV, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Elizabeth, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Idoine Leybourne el 28 de diciembre de 1295, en Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 3 de marzo de 1322, en su pueblo, a la edad de 40 años.
